title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for entry identity, idempotent upsert, revisions, and deletion semantics and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for idempotent versioned entry ingestion model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Entry Key Scope · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Entry Key Scope
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 4569 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Reingestion Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Reingestion Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 6188 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove replay, reordered items, duplicate IDs, corrected dates, moved links, and partial failure converge on the same admitted state.
